---
title: "Fit Options"
---

The optional `[fit_options]` block configures the estimation method and
optimizer settings. All keys have defaults, so this block can be omitted
entirely for a simple FOCE fit.

## Syntax

```
[fit_options]
  key = value
```

## General options

| Key | Values | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `method` | `foce`, `focei`, `saem`, `gn`, `gn_hybrid`, or list e.g. `[saem, focei]` | `focei` | Estimation method |
| `maxiter` | integer | `500` | Maximum outer loop iterations |
| `covariance` | `true`, `false` | `true` | Compute covariance matrix and standard errors |
| `optimizer` | see table below | `slsqp` | Outer-loop optimization algorithm |
| `inner_maxiter` | integer | `200` | Max inner (EBE) optimizer iterations per subject |
| `inner_tol` | float | `1e-4` | Gradient-norm convergence tolerance for inner EBE optimizer |
| `mu_referencing` | `true`, `false` | `true` | Auto-detect and apply mu-referencing from `[individual_parameters]` |
| `bloq_method` | `drop`, `m3` | `drop` | BLOQ handling: `drop` ignores censored rows; `m3` uses Beal's M3 likelihood |
| `iov_column` | string | — | Name of the occasion column in the dataset (required for IOV models) |
| `optimizer_trace` | `true`, `false` | `false` | Write per-iteration CSV to `/tmp/ferx_trace_<pid>_<ts>.csv` |
| `n_starts` | integer | `1` | Number of independent fits from perturbed initial values; the run with the lowest OFV is returned. `1` disables multi-start. |
| `start_sigma` | float | `0.3` | Log-space perturbation size applied to theta for starts 1..n. Start 0 always uses the exact initial values from the model file. |
| `multi_start_seed` | integer | — | RNG seed for reproducible multi-start perturbations |
| `max_unconverged_frac` | float (0–1) | `0.3` | Fraction of subjects allowed to have unconverged inner loops without triggering a warning. Raise when fitting complex models with sparse data. |
| `min_obs_for_convergence_check` | integer | `1` | Minimum number of observations a subject must have before its inner convergence is counted toward `max_unconverged_frac`. |

## Estimation methods

### FOCE

```
method = foce
```

First-Order Conditional Estimation. Linearises the model around the EBE η̂
and evaluates residual variance at η = 0. Fast and reliable for additive-error
models.

### FOCEI (default)

```
method = focei
```

FOCE with Interaction. Evaluates residual variance at η̂ rather than η = 0,
capturing the dependence of residual error on individual predictions. More
accurate than FOCE when the error model is proportional or combined.

### SAEM

```
method = saem
```

Stochastic Approximation EM. Uses Metropolis-Hastings sampling instead of MAP
optimisation for random effects. More robust to local minima; recommended for
complex models with many random effects. **Not supported with IOV.**

### Gauss-Newton

```
method = gn
method = gn_hybrid
```

Gauss-Newton with BHHH Hessian approximation and Levenberg-Marquardt damping.
`gn` typically converges in 10–30 iterations; `gn_hybrid` uses GN to find
the basin and then polishes with FOCEI. Use `gn` for fast feedback during
model development.

### Chained methods

Pass a list to run stages sequentially, each starting from the previous
converged parameters:

```
method = [saem, focei]
```

A SAEM→FOCEI chain is recommended for complex models: SAEM finds the basin
robustly, FOCEI polishes to the MLE and produces a valid covariance matrix.

## Optimizer choices

| Optimizer | Algorithm | Recommended for |
|---|---|---|
| `slsqp` | NLopt SLSQP (default) | General use |
| `bfgs` | Built-in BFGS quasi-Newton | When NLopt is unavailable |
| `lbfgs` | Built-in L-BFGS | Large parameter spaces |
| `nlopt_lbfgs` | NLopt L-BFGS | Alternative L-BFGS |
| `mma` | NLopt Method of Moving Asymptotes | Constrained problems |
| `bobyqa` | NLopt BOBYQA — derivative-free | Noisy/non-smooth objectives where FD gradients are unreliable |
| `trust_region` | Newton trust-region with Steihaug CG | Well-conditioned problems with small parameter count |

**`bobyqa`** does not use gradients — robust to small discontinuities in the
FOCE surface caused by EBE re-estimation, but converges more slowly on smooth
problems.

**`trust_region`** uses an AD-based gradient and a BHHH approximate Hessian
(`H ≈ 4 Σ gᵢgᵢᵀ`), which is always positive semi-definite and costs no extra
OFV evaluations beyond the gradient. The Steihaug CG budget defaults to
`ceil(sqrt(n_params))` (typically 5 for standard NLME models); override with
`steihaug_max_iters` if needed.

## SAEM-specific options

| Key | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `n_exploration` | `150` | Phase 1 iterations (step size γ = 1) |
| `n_convergence` | `250` | Phase 2 iterations (step size γ = 1/k) |
| `n_mh_steps` | `3` | Metropolis-Hastings steps per subject per iteration |
| `adapt_interval` | `50` | Iterations between MH step-size adaptation |
| `seed` | `12345` | RNG seed for reproducibility |

## Global search

```
global_search = true
```

Runs NLopt CRS2-LM (Controlled Random Search with Local Mutation) as a
gradient-free pre-search before the local optimizer. Samples within parameter
bounds; the local optimizer starts from the best point found. Useful for
poorly-identified models or when the local optimizer lands in degenerate basins.

| Key | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `global_search` | `false` | Enable CRS2-LM pre-search |
| `global_maxeval` | `200 * (n_params + 1)` | Max OFV evaluations during global search |

Requires a full NLopt build — a warning is emitted if CRS2-LM is unavailable.

## SIR (Sampling Importance Resampling)

SIR provides non-parametric parameter uncertainty estimates as an optional
post-estimation step. Requires `covariance = true`.

| Key | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sir` | `false` | Enable SIR |
| `sir_samples` | `1000` | Number of proposal samples (M) |
| `sir_resamples` | `250` | Number of resampled vectors (m) |
| `sir_seed` | `12345` | RNG seed |

See [Estimation — SIR](../ferx-core/estimation.qmd#sampling-importance-resampling-sir) for details.

## Parameter scaling and EBE convergence guard

| Key | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `scale_params` | `true` | Scale packed parameters to O(1) before optimizer. Improves conditioning when log-transformed parameters span orders of magnitude. |
| `max_unconverged_frac` | `0.1` | Fraction of subjects (with ≥ `min_obs_for_convergence_check` observations) allowed to have unconverged EBEs before the step is rejected (OFV = ∞). Set to `1.0` to disable. |
| `min_obs_for_convergence_check` | `2` | Subjects with fewer observations are excluded from the convergence-guard check. |
| `steihaug_max_iters` | adaptive | Max CG iterations for the trust-region Steihaug subproblem (`optimizer = trust_region` only). Default uses `ceil(sqrt(n_params))` clamped to at least 5 — typically 5 for standard NLME models. Set explicitly to pin the budget (e.g. `steihaug_max_iters = 20`). |

## Options that don't apply to the selected method

If you set an option the chosen method doesn't consume (e.g. `n_convergence`
with `method = focei`), ferx emits a warning listing the option and the keys
that *are* available. The option is ignored — the fit still proceeds.

For a chained fit (`method = [saem, focei]`), an option is kept if it applies
to *any* stage in the chain, so SAEM and FOCEI keys can be mixed without warnings.

## Examples

Standard FOCE with defaults:

```
[fit_options]
  method     = foce
  maxiter    = 300
  covariance = true
```

Multi-start FOCEI (8 parallel starts, reproducible):

```
[fit_options]
  method           = focei
  covariance       = true
  n_starts         = 8
  start_sigma      = 0.3
  multi_start_seed = 42
```

FOCEI with global search:

```
[fit_options]
  method        = focei
  maxiter       = 500
  covariance    = true
  global_search = true
```

SAEM with custom settings:

```
[fit_options]
  method        = saem
  n_exploration = 200
  n_convergence = 300
  n_mh_steps    = 5
  seed          = 42
  covariance    = true
```

SAEM → FOCEI chain:

```
[fit_options]
  method     = [saem, focei]
  covariance = true
```

FOCEI with SIR uncertainty:

```
[fit_options]
  method        = focei
  covariance    = true
  sir           = true
  sir_samples   = 1000
  sir_resamples = 250
  sir_seed      = 42
```

Derivative-free BOBYQA fit:

```
[fit_options]
  method        = foce
  optimizer     = bobyqa
  maxiter       = 300
  inner_maxiter = 100
  inner_tol     = 1e-6
```

IOV model:

```
[fit_options]
  method     = focei
  iov_column = OCC
  covariance = true
```

Enable optimizer trace with EBE guard:

```
[fit_options]
  method                        = focei
  optimizer_trace               = true
  max_unconverged_frac          = 0.5
  min_obs_for_convergence_check = 3
```

M3 BLOQ handling:

```
[fit_options]
  method      = focei
  bloq_method = m3
  covariance  = true
```

## Optimizer trace

When `optimizer_trace = true`, a CSV is written to `/tmp/ferx_trace_<pid>_<ts>.csv`
and the path is stored in `FitResult::trace_path`. Each row is one outer iteration.
See [Output Reference — Optimizer trace CSV](../ferx-core/output.qmd#optimizer-trace-csv)
for the full column reference.

In R:

```r
fit <- ferx_fit(model, data, optimizer_trace = TRUE)
tr  <- ferx_trace(fit)       # read the CSV
ferx_plot_trace(fit)         # two-panel OFV + convergence plot
```
